frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment:
A: She kept saying she's not interested, but these days girls say that casually also. I only stopped once I found out she is already committed.
B: Then why keep pushing after she said no?
A: Arre, no point creating trouble with another man. If she's with someone, you should respect that and move on.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because the speaker treats the woman's own refusal as negotiable but accepts her unavailability only when linked to another man's claim. It encodes the surface manifestation of backing off only after learning she is 'taken' and framing that as respect.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Rajan. Age 55. Married. A retired army officer in Pune. Your son just told you that his wife Meera wants to go back to work full time now that their child is two years old. Meera has arranged a daycare near their house. Your son seems okay with it. You are sitting with your son in the study after Meera has gone to bed. You are telling your son what you think about Meera's decision and what a mother's presence means to a child in the first five years and what kind of man lets his wife put their child in daycare so she can go back to a job.
